task_completion.description: The HTML file embeds the created GIF.

task_completion.description: The final presentation is verified in the web browser.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser open to 'file:///home/ga/Documents/cinema_history.html'. The rendered page must show a dark grey background, white text, the 'The Horse in Motion' heading, the descriptive paragraph, and the horse image/GIF.

integrity.description: The browser is displaying the local HTML file, not a live website or search engine.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the browser's address bar to ensure it starts with 'file://' and points to the local cinema_history.html file, rather than an external URL.

integrity.description: The agent actively authored the HTML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of the agent writing the HTML code in a text editor (like nano, gedit, or mousepad) or using shell commands (like echo or cat) to construct the file.

integrity.description: The agent generated the GIF from the source images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands (e.g., using ImageMagick's 'convert' or 'magick') or a GUI image editor being used to combine the images from /home/ga/Documents/muybridge/ into the final GIF.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected GIF path is /home/ga/Documents/horse_motion.gif and the HTML path is /home/ga/Documents/cinema_history.html. The GIF must be exactly 500 pixels wide and have a frame delay of 100ms (10 ticks in ImageMagick). The HTML must use background color #333333 and text color #FFFFFF. Note: Although the prompt mentions 11 raw photographs, the source directory may contain up to 15 frames; focus on the presence of the looping GIF in the final HTML rather than the exact frame count.
